refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how to achieve this, only what is required. Therefore, it is atomic and does not need further decomposition.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design redundant communication paths to provide failover and reduce effective packet loss
Incorporate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback to teleoperation control system
Apply data compression and prioritization to optimize bandwidth usage and reduce packet loss
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol adjustments and teleoperation control timing
Dependency on network condition monitoring accuracy
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical solution concept that can be modularized independently from teleoperation control logic, supporting decoupling. Potential coupling arises if network monitoring tightly integrates with control system timing, but this can be managed with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be sufficiently monitored in real-time to enable adaptive adjustments
Risk that adaptive protocol complexity may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes customer-defined packet loss limit is achievable under agreed network conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the interface and data exchange requirements between the adaptive communication protocol and teleoperation control system to ensure modular independence and support verification of packet loss compliance.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network communication management and protocol adaptation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends communication performance data and status updates

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to physical and logical network layers for sending and receiving data packets

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to network handling; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not manage protocol adaptation; would cause high diffusion.
Teleoperation depends on communication but protocol adaptation is not its responsibility; moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate protocol adaptation logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid cross-module diffusion.
If adaptive protocol requires safety constraints, coordination with Safety Services may increase coupling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed; DP is well contained within NGHE Solution. Ensure clear interface definitions to minimize cross-module dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters, ensuring packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit, thus fulfilling the teleoperation communication quality requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid cross-module diffusion.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety constraints may increase coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion, making it the appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP. The module containment score is high (8) with low diffusion (2), indicating good encapsulation of the DP within this IM.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring compliance with the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, as this module is responsible for network communication management and protocol adaptation, aligning well with the FR.

recommended_revisions: No revision needed; DP is well contained within NGHE Solution. Ensure clear interface definitions to minimize cross-module dependencies and manage potential coupling with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution IM with high cohesion and minimal diffusion, and the FR is clearly allocated. The recommended revisions are minor interface clarifications rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition ensures clear responsibility and manageable coupling, supporting a stable design without further sub-FR derivation at this time.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol DP fully satisfies the teleoperation communication quality FR.
Interface definitions can be refined to manage cross-module dependencies effectively.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules may require ongoing interface management.
Real-time data dependencies could introduce integration complexity.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data showing packet loss remains ≤1% under operational conditions.
Interface specifications demonstrating minimal diffusion and clear boundaries.
